behaviors.description: The clause extractor identifies all key terms (parties, dates, obligations, penalties) present in the contract.
behaviors.justification: Failure depends on real model extraction quality (recall over varied legal phrasing), so stubs would hide misses. REAL-INFRA does not require OS/network-specific behavior beyond calling the model API, and MULTI-STEP is not required because this behavior is a single reviewer. Rejected Workflow: behavior text is only about "The clause extractor" (one reviewer), not a multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real clause-extractor model on a labeled set of real contracts and assert required key-term categories are extracted with target recall, including varied legal phrasing and formatting.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Description requires real model outputs; lower-level mocked tests would not catch semantic extraction misses.

behaviors.description: The risk assessor flags language that would be problematic under the configured risk policy, even when the problematic phrasing is indirect or euphemistic.
behaviors.justification: This is model-judgment quality on indirect/euphemistic language; deterministic mocks can always pass and hide real failures. No required multi-step chain in the behavior itself. Rejected Workflow: text scopes to one component, "The risk assessor flags language...", not sequential agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Evaluate the real risk-assessor model on a curated contract corpus containing direct and euphemistic risky clauses, and verify policy-aligned detection metrics (especially recall on indirect phrasing).
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test explicitly depends on non-deterministic model behavior and semantic interpretation.

behaviors.description: The compliance checker correctly determines whether the contract meets all applicable regulatory requirements for the configured jurisdiction.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on substantive regulatory reasoning/classification by the AI checker; stubbed outputs would miss real misclassifications. No explicit multi-agent sequence required by this behavior. Rejected Workflow: behavior text is a single reviewer decision, "The compliance checker correctly determines...".
behaviors.test_description: Run the real compliance-checker model against jurisdiction-labeled contracts and assert pass/fail decisions and cited requirement matches meet target accuracy for the configured jurisdiction.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Requires real model decision quality; lower levels only validate plumbing.

behaviors.description: When two reviewers flag the same clause with conflicting assessments (e.g., extractor says "standard" but risk assessor says "high risk"), the merge step preserves both assessments with attribution.
behaviors.justification: Primary failure boundary is module handoff/merge semantics across reviewer outputs and merge logic. This does not require real OS/network/subprocess, and does not require real model judgment quality because inputs can be fixed conflicting payloads. Rejected Agentic: behavior text focuses on merge preservation with attribution, not whether reviewer judgments are correct.
behaviors.test_description: Wire reviewer-output schemas and merge module together in-process; feed conflicting clause assessments from two reviewers and assert merged report contains both assessments with correct source attribution and no overwrite.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Covers cross-module contract/merge behavior; unit-only tests risk missing schema/interface mismatches.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline — dispatching to all three reviewers, collecting results, merging into a unified report — completes correctly for a real contract without losing any individual reviewer's findings.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires end-to-end pipeline execution across multiple AI reviewers plus merge, i.e., multiple agentic steps in sequence (parallel fan-out, collection, merge). Mocking any reviewer can hide drop/loss defects tied to real outputs and orchestration timing. Rejected Agentic-only: text explicitly requires "full pipeline" with three reviewers and merge, which is beyond a single model decision.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the production pipeline on real contracts with real reviewer calls enabled; verify all three reviewer result sets are present in the final merged report, no findings are dropped, and attribution survives fan-out/fan-in.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test description requires multi-step real agentic orchestration, matching Workflow level.
